text.title: DM Personalisation Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every DM Personalisation Template application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Each DM MUST contain a 1-line personalisation citing a specific public artefact (post, project, comment) from the prospect. Template-only DMs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Generic DMs are filtered as spam; per-prospect citation lifts response rate by 5-10x.

rule.statement: DMs MUST NOT use SDR clichés ('Hope you're well', 'Quick question', 'Saw your profile and...'). Cliché-heavy DMs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Cliché openers trigger the spam pattern; humans recognise and ignore them instantly.

rule.statement: Batches MUST be 10 prospects (not 50, not 100). Bulk batches are rejected.
rule.rationale: Personalisation quality decays past 10; bulk batches devolve to templates.

rule.statement: Each DM MUST contain one explicit ask matching the relationship stage. Asks-vague DMs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Vague DMs do not convert; an explicit ask gives the prospect a path.

rule.statement: Output MUST log per-prospect response status (no-reply / reply / converted) so future batches can iterate.
rule.rationale: Without tracking the personalisation pattern cannot be improved.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
